items.source: MEDDPICC_2_0__c (blockers), updated 2026-05-15; partially discussed via private-cloud/on-prem AI options in the 2026-01-13 call but not confirmed as compliant

metrics.narrative: SAMI's pain is well articulated qualitatively (fragmented/manual supplier processes, need for one auditable platform covering local content, SDP and IPP), but no quantified value case specific to SAMI has been captured. The only quantified reference point discussed is Zycus's own ST Engineering customer example (a 24-month, ERP-aligned phased rollout) raised in the 13 Jan 2026 call - that is Zycus's case study, not SAMI's business case.

champion.narrative: No champion is confirmed. MEDDPICC_2_0__c explicitly states this is a 'very arms-length process' and that Franck Devienne, despite positive conversations, 'doesn't fit the champion role.' No SAMI-side individual has been observed advocating for Zycus internally in any read call or note.

competition.narrative: Four named competitors are tracked: SAP Ariba (SAP/IBM ecosystem relationship, though MEDDPICC notes the buyer 'clearly' finds them 'unliked' on relationship grounds while still probing Zycus on integration parity), Ivalua (heavy customization/localization angle), GEP Worldwide (heavy services support), and Coupa (strong aerospace & defense customer base). SAMI confirmed Zycus among a shortlist of 3 as of 2 Mar 2026, and the 3 Jul 2026 Next Step records the rep's read that Zycus's demo-stage position 'was good.'

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly and consistently stated: fragmented/manual supplier-facing processes across onboarding, qualification, sourcing and development, lack of transparency and standardized supplier data across HOLDCO/OPCOs, and the need to support direct/defense procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP on one auditable platform. This was reinforced in the 13 Jan 2026 call discussion of SAMI's manual, ERP-disconnected current state.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifacts (MSA, Order Form, SOW) have been referenced anywhere in the read evidence. The deal is still in the RFP-evaluation/Shortlisted stage; paper process cannot begin until SAMI's stated final decision round (mid-to-end July 2026) concludes.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC_2_0__c (updated 15 May 2026) names Franck Devienne, Senior Procurement Advisor, as economic buyer, which is treated as authoritative identification. However the same field notes budget is 'centrally held, but Franck and team will determine the award decision' - meaning Franck holds process authority, not confirmed personal budget sign-off. Franck attended the 23 Jun 2026 live demo (fdevienne@sami.com.sa), but no CFO/CPO-level executive has joined any read call.

decision_process.narrative: The process (RFI -> Innovation Contest -> RFP issued to shortlisted vendors -> clarification questions -> RFP response -> presentation -> evaluation -> final negotiations/compliance check -> award) is corroborated step-by-step by the actual timeline: NDA 15 Dec 2025, RFI 16 Dec 2025 (due 24 Jan 2026), Innovation Contest presentations 22-26 Feb 2026, RFP issued 13 Mar 2026, ~600 RFP clarifications submitted 13 May 2026, live demo 23 Jun 2026, and a stated final decision round around mid-July 2026 with evaluations concluding end of July 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and detailed: functional/technical and cybersecurity compliance, SAP/S/4HANA integration, A&D/direct-procurement/MRO fit, local content/LCGPA/SDP/IPP fit, KSA implementation/support capability, knowledge transfer, implementation plan, a filled compliance matrix, and commercial response. These are corroborated by the 23 Jun 2026 demo, where defense-specific controls (classified-material lineage, trade/FARS compliance, consignment/MRP integration with S/4HANA) were raised as live evaluation questions.

items.summary: Yousef (SAMI) followed up on Franck's June 22 request, noting the vendor proposals and AEC hosting info were promised 'early this week' but still hadn't arrived by Thursday — a pointed nudge signaling SAMI's patience is wearing thin.

items.name: Carl's status update on SI partners + revised DC slide
items.summary: Carl replied same-day with the AEC data warehouse slide update and a partner-by-partner status: EY nearly done reconciling teams, KPMG committing to finish in 1-2 days, and Accenture again declining to bid alone (wants to pair with another ecosystem partner SAMI hasn't approved); final implementation commercials are on hold until all proposals and joint costs are finalized.

day_summary.overall: SAMI's Yousef chased Zycus (again) for the overdue SI vendor proposals and AEC hosting details, having expected them 'early this week' but still empty-handed by Thursday. Carl finally responded same-day with a substantive status update: EY's proposal is nearly finalized, KPMG has committed to finishing in 1-2 days, but Accenture has again refused to bid solo (insisting on partnering with another ecosystem vendor SAMI doesn't want). Carl also flagged that final implementation commercials can't be locked until all SI proposals land and joint costs are optimized — so the deal remains stalled on Zycus's side pending partner deliverables, with SAMI now visibly frustrated at the slipping timeline.

Two Zycus-side deliverables (written defense-compliance responses; revised SI-partner quotes) have been outstanding since 23 Jun 2026 (~15 days as of today), stalling the run-up to that decision. If SAMI decides mid-to-late July, the 29 Aug 2026 close leaves only 4-6 weeks for SI-partner-mediated commercial negotiation, defense/InfoSec compliance close-out, and contracting on a $2.2M enterprise A&D deal.

No champion exists inside SAMI beyond the arms-length gatekeeper Franck Devienne; with the award decision expected mid-July 2026, cultivate an internal advocate among the technical/A&D stakeholders who joined the 23 Jun demo.

Zycus is currently the bottleneck on its own committed SI implementation proposal (KPMG/EY), chased three times by the buyer since 16 Jun 2026; closing this fast protects the 'good' technical position already earned in the demo.

Close both overdue 23 Jun deliverables (defense-compliance written answers, revised SI-routed quotes) this week; SAMI's stated end-of-July evaluation close leaves little room for slow responses to reopen the file.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal's highest-leverage next moves are closing the executive-access gap, clearing overdue technical proof points before the buyer's decision window closes, and validating Zycus's AI deployment model against SAMI's data-residency restriction.

customer_preference.evidence: The only positive read on standing is rep-side ('our position it was good', Next_Step__c, 3 Jul 2026); no buyer-voiced preference statement for Zycus over the other 3 shortlisted vendors is present in the read evidence.

competitive_position.summary: Four named competitors are in the field for SAMI's S2P RFP, all still active as of the most recent evidence (MEDDPICC 2.0, updated 15 May 2026), with SAMI having confirmed a shortlist of 3 finalists on 2 Mar 2026 (composition of which 3 remain is not confirmed in the read evidence). SAP Ariba is the strongest threat on technical (S/4HANA integration) grounds even though the buyer reportedly dislikes Ariba on relationship grounds; Coupa's aerospace & defense customer base is a direct fit-relevant claim for this deal.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: SAMI_S2P is a live, engaged Shortlisted opportunity (1 of a confirmed 3 finalists as of 2 Mar 2026) inside a long defense-sector RFP, with a genuine two-way buyer email exchange 2 days ago (7 Jul 2026) and a stated mid-to-end-July 2026 decision window. The deal's real exposure is structural, not activity-based: no economic buyer or champion has personally engaged Zycus beyond the arms-length Franck Devienne relationship, and SAP Ariba's S/4HANA integration edge remains unanswered.

deal_risk.text: S/4HANA integration parity with SAP Ariba is unproven; Zycus offered iSaaS middleware/adapters at the 23 Jun 2026 demo but has not yet supplied the recent Saudi/S/4HANA implementation examples the buyer requested.

Defense-specific compliance questions (trade/FARS compliance, classified-material lineage, third-party validation, consignment/MRP integration) raised by the A&D evaluator at the 23 Jun 2026 demo remain unanswered.
No confirmed economic buyer or champion has personally engaged Zycus above Franck Devienne; budget authority beyond 'centrally held' is unmapped.

The 29 Aug 2026 close date is not credible given the buyer's own stated mid-to-end-July 2026 decision window plus the unstarted contracting path (commercial negotiation, InfoSec/legal, MSA/SOW, signature) for a defense-sector deal.

win_position.text: SAMI confirmed Zycus among the final 3 shortlisted vendors as of 2 Mar 2026, and the rep's 3 Jul 2026 Next Step records that 'our position it was good' from the demo-stage evaluation, with only isolated technical queries outstanding.

No economic buyer or champion has directly engaged Zycus: Franck Devienne is named EB in MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 15 May 2026) but is explicitly noted as not fitting the champion role, and award authority sits with 'Franck and team' centrally rather than a single named executive.

SAP Ariba is the leading competitive risk: SAMI runs S/4HANA and Ariba carries an SAP/IBM ecosystem edge; the Saudi/S/4HANA integration reference examples the buyer requested at the 23 Jun 2026 demo remain outstanding as of 3 Jul 2026.

The 'zero AI functionality accepted' data-residency blocker (MEDDPICC 2.0) threatens Zycus's core AI/Merlin differentiation against Ariba, Ivalua and GEP unless an on-prem/private-cloud AI model is validated as compliant.

deal_momentum.text: A genuine two-way buyer touch occurred 7 Jul 2026 (incoming email reply, 2 days before this sweep), following the rep's 3 Jul 2026 outreach on revised quotes and technical queries.

A live SRM demo was delivered 23 Jun 2026 with 12 SAMI-side attendees including the A&D technical evaluator (Ahmed Kamal, alsalam.aero), and the buyer confirmed a final decision round is expected around mid-July 2026.

Several buyer-raised technical items from the 23 Jun 2026 demo (defense-compliance answers, historical-price-view confirmation, Saudi S/4HANA references) remained unanswered as of the 3 Jul 2026 Next Step, over a week later.

customer_commitment.text: SAMI has sustained the evaluation relationship across a 7-month process from the 15 Dec 2025 NDA through RFI, Innovation Contest, RFP (~600 clarifications submitted 13 May 2026), and the 23 Jun 2026 demo, without dropping Zycus from its 3-vendor shortlist.

Commitment observed so far is process-level, not relationship-level: engagement runs through Franck Devienne and the procurement/technical evaluation team, with no CFO/CPO-level sponsor observed on any read call.
